Content Author.error: Element does not have an alt attribute

arrmReferences.task: Informative alternate text is provided for images (i.e. not "spacer" or image file name).
Informative images are described with a clear and meaningful text equivalent (alt attribute or other equivalent means).
Purely decorative images are provided with null alt attribute values (or other equivalent means).
Null alt attribute values are used for images that are already described in text in adjacent page content.
Alt text used for images of text include all relevant text found in the image.

Content Author.rationale: Step 1 override: image-alt inside article context maps to Content Author

arrmReferences.task: Informative alternate text is provided for images (i.e. not "spacer" or image file name).
Informative images are described with a clear and meaningful text equivalent (alt attribute or other equivalent means).
Purely decorative images are provided with null alt attribute values (or other equivalent means).
Null alt attribute values are used for images that are already described in text in adjacent page content.
Alt text used for images of text include all relevant text found in the image.

Themer / Visual Designer.error:
Fix any of the following:
  Element has insufficient color contrast of 2.97 (foreground color: #288bff, background color: #0b3d91, font size: 36.0pt (48px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 3:1
Themer / Visual Designer.suggestedFixLocation: Theme and design system layer (global CSS/components)

arrmReferences.task: Icon fonts used to convey information are provided with a text equivalent.
Background images identified as decorative are implemented as such.
Shape and location are never used as the only way to convey information and relationships between page components.
Users relying on High Contrast themes don't lose information as a result of doing so.
Content is viewable in both portrait and landscape orientation, unless a particular orientation is essential.

Developer.snippet: <input class="radiotab" name="tabs" type="radio" id="consumers" checked="checked" aria-expanded="false" data-once="tab-click">
Developer.error: ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-expanded="false"
Developer.suggestedFixLocation: Custom code/module fixes (templates, JS, backend forms)

arrmReferences.task: Additional content triggered by focus or hover that covers other information can be dismissed by the user.
Additional content triggered by pointer hover does not disappear when trying to move the pointer over it.
Additional content triggered by hover or focus stays visible until purposefully un-triggered, dismissed, or is no longer valid.
All actionable elements can be reached, using only the keyboard.
All active elements can be triggered, using only the keyboard.

Ambiguous.suggestedFixLocation: Manual triage needed (run AI classifier or human review)

Ambiguous.snippet: <button id="global-navigation-trigger--mobile" class="hds-global-menu-toggle menu-toggle menu-mobile-toggle" aria-expanded="false" data-menu-action="open" data-menu-location="left">
Ambiguous.error:
Fix any of the following:
  Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  Element has no title attribute
  Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  Element does not have an explicit <label>
  Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Ambiguous.suggestedFixLocation: Manual triage needed (run AI classifier or human review)

arrmReferences.task: Informative alternate text is provided for images (i.e. not "spacer" or image file name).
Informative images are described with a clear and meaningful text equivalent (alt attribute or other equivalent means).
Purely decorative images are provided with null alt attribute values (or other equivalent means).
Null alt attribute values are used for images that are already described in text in adjacent page content.
Alt text used for images of text include all relevant text found in the image.

arrmReferences.task: Additional content triggered by focus or hover that covers other information can be dismissed by the user.
Additional content triggered by pointer hover does not disappear when trying to move the pointer over it.
Additional content triggered by hover or focus stays visible until purposefully un-triggered, dismissed, or is no longer valid.
All actionable elements can be reached, using only the keyboard.
All active elements can be triggered, using only the keyboard.
